name: ObjectBox
description: ObjectBox is a high-performance on-device database for objects and vectors, designed for mobile, edge, and IoT applications. It uses FlatBuffers for zero-copy serialization and stores data in a single file with ACID transactions. ObjectBox supports vector similarity search for on-device AI workloads (RAG, semantic search) alongside traditional object persistence with relations, queries, and automatic schema migrations. Available across C/C++, Go, Java/Kotlin, Swift, Dart/Flutter, and Python, it runs fully embedded with no server required and optional Data Sync for multi-device coordination.
core_strengths: On-device vector search for local AI, RAG, and semantic similarity
FlatBuffers-based zero-copy serialization with no parsing overhead
ACID transactions with automatic schema migrations and no manual scripts
Embedded-only with no server required, runs in-process on constrained devices
Multi-platform: Android, iOS, Linux, macOS, Windows, and embedded ARM
Object persistence with built-in relations, indexes, and type-safe queries
best_use_cases: On-device AI with local vector search (RAG, semantic similarity)
Mobile apps needing fast local object and vector storage (Android, iOS, Flutter)
Edge/IoT devices with constrained CPU, memory, and power budgets
Offline-first applications with optional cross-device sync
Embedded object database replacing SQLite for performance-sensitive workloads
remarks: ObjectBox is a multi-repo project: objectbox/objectbox-java (main), objectbox/objectbox-go, objectbox/objectbox-python, objectbox/objectbox-c, objectbox/objectbox-swift, objectbox/objectbox-dart. The core database engine is written in C/C++ with language-specific bindings. Go and Dart/Flutter require code generation (go generate / build_runner). Python vector search is in alpha as of v4.0.0. ObjectBox Sync (commercial) enables multi-device data synchronization with an optional sync server.
